entries.text: It is completely dark here. No light enters, no light escapes. The ground beneath the HELLPIERCERS is wet. A cold white moon hangs lifelessly in the distance. On it, a strange network of ruins, fissures, and craters tell a morose story. The soul of the Demiurge, its true soul, the one you drove a sword through at the mountain of humanity's birth, is in there. Every atom in your body knows it. Any exertion of will in this room parts the dark ocean below the HELLPIERCER, a diamond core rising to meet them. Unlock the following Vehicle Options: Personalization - Exalted Idiot Creator Of All That is Vehicle gains the personality and memories of the Demiurge, the naive monster who wrought all pain in the universe in an ignorant search to emulate its mothers love. Upgrade - Wings of Infinite Light The vehicle gains collapsible wings of emerald-purple-golden light. These wings allow semi-effortless vertical ascension, and can themselves open temporary rifts in the local reality matrix. Consume 1 Fuel when deployed. Grant the vehicle the temporary tag Active-Arrested Pseudo-Evolution Catalyst until returning to DIS. Upgrade - Final Rage Directing Giga-Art When combined with a ritual catalyst such as a fruit of wisdom or a legendary arm, the vehicle begins a cataclysmic ascension rite that transforms the vehicle into an indomitable giant of darkness. The ritual ends with its total annihilation, and the annihilation of an area roughly 2 Speed across on the STRATCOM. This ascension fundamentally changes the rules of the local reality matrix. Decide among your table how this plays out.

Somehow, a building from the ogdoad has laid under the catacombs of Hell for millenia. Oddly colored flesh, bright hot red, is gilded with immaculately polished gold. Dazzling terminals sprawl endless flowing Heaven-Code, sentient programming endlessly pursuing some unknown project. The bulk of the vault is a massive pit holding a pale humanoid giant bound by golden chains. Its head has been removed, replaced with an enormous golden Lion mask caught in a vicious snarl. High above the creature is an unnerving halo, swirling with unidentifiable soul-matter.

Every surface of the vault is covered in a bizarre, beautiful mycelium. The strange non-Euclidean structures shift with an unseen, unheard, irregular heartbeat that sends pulses of golden light through the silvery flesh of the fungi. The ground underfoot is soft and yielding, the smell earthy but with a fizzy hint of divinity. The walls are marked with ancient, decaying anti-divinity seals in a variety of demonic languages. Several towers of mycelium dot the area, and when communed with readily accept a spinther into them for communion. Whoever returns holds the One Last Minute Legendary Arm. Only beings with souls would find themselves in such a cell, and only beings who have tested the bounds of divinity warrant this security.

Entry into this vault catapults the wanderers into an incomprehensible miasma of souls. Physicality washes away. All sense of stable space, even the understanding of it, struggles and fails against an onslaught of a stranger space. Lives, goals, ideals, memories, dreams, entire persons play out across impossible shapes that transgress through what little body exists of the entrant. A HELLPIERCER could escape this, and would carry some mote of value with them, but extracting this many souls from a chamber of guf requires specialized equipment. Why were they put here in this prison-within-a-prison in the first place?

A large frog perches within a jar. Its vessel is adorned in countless scribes of admiration. The creature gurgles within its jar for a moment before handing you a small red capsule. At any time you may break this capsule and receive any locked weapon until the end of the next TACCOM encounter.

There is an obelisk here, marked in a language nobody in the group understands at first. When touched, it reveals itself to be a collection of souls in an extra-dimensional bunker, hiding from something. They select one HELLPIERCER and grant them the first step of the Halcyon Process. There are seven more steps to uncover.

This vault has already been looted, but in the center rest remains of an infernal construction engine. If players are able to repair its systems, it constructs one building in DIS for free before the souls that make up its machinery are returned to their rightful forms. Name them. Get to know them a little.

A single, plain jar rests in the center of an otherwise empty and bland tomb. When taken back to DIS and researched, it reveals itself to be a Demon Binding Pot, and adds the following action to the HELLPIERCERS abilities: Seal As a Main Action, select an active enemy within this TACCOM encounter and place a Countdown:3 on them. At the end of the countdown, that enemy is permanently sealed within the pot and can only be released by breaking it. If broken, the enemy delivers one of their attacks at a target of the players choosing before vanishing forever. Discuss with the table how the attack works beforehand.

As the locks on this vault roll back into hidden dimensions, the door unfurls like a flower then turns rotten, its petals sloughing off into wet lumps of gooey mess. A red mist seeps from the open doorway, followed quickly by a cyclone of hellish miasma. The vault is empty but for a half-ruined effigy of a twisted Archon, half-fused with some demonic creature. Before the next session, sit down with the table and develop an entirely new faction representing one of the Archon’s Hellguard fused with demons. Create at minimum a Faction Boss, a miniboss, and at least one minion. These creatures may be deployed by the GM during any future missions.

The vault contains only the flayed torso of some immense creature. Its blood is gold, its skin a shattered mirror of silver and bronze. The creature's broken ribcage houses an emerald roughly the size of a car. Touching it reveals to all present a ritual for diving their pneuma into it and the machine needed to do so. Its representative begins to haunt the party, seen on distant walkways, meeting with moribund characters.

Past the threshold of the vault the HELLPIERCERS forms—their golden skin, their nouswork armor—melts into a stunning mundanity. Their flesh prickles in the cold, their eyes strain against the dark, and the thrumming call of power behind their minds ebbs into a low buzz. Here, they are as Human as they have ever felt. The center of the room holds a chamber with a primordial Armor, forged by an anonymous, long dead demon artisan from the corpse of an undocumented Archon. It has the following stats. YAO Speed: 5 Special Movement: Keppler-Aristotelian Unified Graviton Rite At any point in your movement, you may place a Void Attractor tile. By default, the tile has a Burst:1 range, Attractor:1 strength, and must be placed within Range:2 of your current location. You may spend any or all of your remaining Speed to increase any of these values. You can dismiss this tile at any point during your turn. You may only have one Void Attractor tile active at a time. Armor Action: Particle Interchangeability Module - As a Support Action, you may change the direction of the Void Attractor tile’s attraction, changing its Pull effect to a Push. Using this ability also makes your Attractor tile affect any damage patterns that fall into its Burst range. Each time it is worn, it regains one more of its memories of Heaven, of ruling here in Hell, and of the pathway to its own liberation. It will pursue this goal relentlessly.

The vault appears from the outside as a treasure trove of Hell’s wealth. The arrayed riches of some elder faction lay piled atop strange weapons and inscrutable ritual aids. Upon entering, this illusion fades away. This bunker contains books, tablets, scrolls, and rudimentary technology preserving the work of Edenite Monks who led the Hosanna protocol, the effort to carry the knowledge of ascension through the thousand years of fire. Those who died forged this safe vault in Hell and continued their divine-beyond-divine mission. Their work is impenetrable, and changes focus every few centuries as the monks set their wisdom against archons, demons or internally, seeking some escape from Hell. Select one of the following rituals to pursue and determine the catalyst, the archonic source, and the hylic resonance needed to complete it: Ephemeral Binding Rites Allows a single Legendary Arm to be made a permanent addition to the squad’s weapon options. Unfettered Fifth Truth-Defining Ascension Ritual Uncovers a further layer of Truth to the universe, enlightening the HELLPIERCERS beyond their cosmic liberation, past the Hebdomas and the Monad, to a realm of complete understanding. Completing this ritual requires a living Faction Boss, the fused cores of an ascended human and some other alien power, and a weapon capable of breaking the Keter-Lokantara barrier. It immediately annihilates all the active factions and unmoors local reality. Discuss with the table what happens next.

A single plinth, surrounded by a small moat of glittering silver water direct from the abyssal ocean of the Demiurge. Crossing it grants HELLPIERCERS one memory from before the great forgetting. The plinth holds the embryonic form of a nascent Demon Lord, its crown still in flux, its single red eye searing a vengeful rune into the eye of anyone brave enough to stare back. This is only part of an animus; it yearns to fuse with power, to trigger something magnificent and obliterative. It dreams of a new status quo, and its dreams bleed into any HELLPIERCER who stands close. Grant one player the following ability: Iron-Fist Prophecy Technique As a Support Action, once per TACCOM select one enemy, then declare which HELLPIERCER they will target next. That enemy cannot make any attack besides one that targets that HELLPIERCER until it fulfills this prophecy.

Opening the ornate door of this vault triggers a hidden mechanism, sending the entire room spiraling upward. It drills through ten thousand layers of history to crest within DIS itself. Within are thousands of scrolls, carvings, tapestries, tablets, tomes, memory-coded bacterium, and audio logs cataloging every failed prophecy there has ever been. Whenever you spend a few hours here, write down 3 things that will absolutely never, ever happen. Should any of these three things come to pass, rewrite events such that they do not.

This chamber is as close to Earth as you have felt since you walked the hero's mimesis ritual back home. The vault resembles the dionysian chambers where you first learned to see the hidden path. Time stops outside of this chamber, you can watch motes of reality juddering and shivering in place past the threshold. The air here is heavy with some imitation of Hyper-LSD, its telltale camphor scent singing your nostrils. Meditate here and share your experience. The one of you who spends the longest here gains the following tag: Psychopharmikos Mantling the demonic perversion of the divine acids has fractalized your nous and given you paths beyond the road ahead. At any time, you may bring any number of people back to the hall you were enthroned, but you can’t leave with more than you carried in with you. You also see the waking lines of hyper-reality, fueling demonic security systems and defenses through their backdoor to Psi-Space, and may manipulate them with enough time and space for a suitable ritual.

Gnarled trees and coiled vines force a tempered approach to the center of this vault. A dome of green grass is crowned with an impossible tree, one of swirling golden boughs and emerald and ruby leaves which clink and tinkle in the non-existent wind. Strange fruits hang from its branches, each with an alien face. A great lion-headed serpent lies sleeping around the tree. Upon approaching, an androgynous figure clad in gold emerges, calling itself Adam-Eve. It offers you a weapon. Decide together what the cost will be.

This chamber is designed around a number of sapphire and garnet sarcophagi—one for each member of the squad. Resting in these grants the sleeper inescapable prophetic dreams, full of potent images of crowns, of a white moon and a broken red planet, of the rotting corpse of the Demiurge and a million demons marching through his watery grave. One of you awakens holding a weapon. The rest of you know the shape of a terrifying apocalypse to come.

Stepping into this vault is like being in the center of every loudspeaker and subwoofer in the world, blasting a wall of noise that threatens to unmake your body and soul. Your A T Field flares, layering upon itself, separating you from your relationships, your connections, all but your nous, spinther, and pneuma. This is what it is to be a demon. Within these ironclad walls you are a master of your own psyche, but the barriers between yourself and others feels unbreakable. A weapon beckons from the raised platform in the center of this room, where the noise is strongest.

Some sort of long-abandoned demonic spying system. Tendrils of techno-organic material twist and coil around a room that appears to be breathing, thinking, feeling. Any one of these tendrils will connect you to a listening station somewhere in deep space, monitoring the comings and goings of Enlightened Humanity's vast population, dancing their ineffable routines through the star clouds of the outerworlds. Their music is transcendent. Perhaps that’s why this place was abandoned in the end. The party gains the following ability: Truth-Obliteration Dragon-Ear Ritual Tower After a successful mission, but before returning to DIS, place a beacon at your current location on the STRATCOM map. You may spend this beacon to fully embody a sentient being within 1 Speed of that location for up to 24 hours. You gain some of that being’s recent memories, their mannerisms, their drives, their will, and their goals. You cannot act in a way unbecoming for that being, but you retain all knowledge gained during your excursion. You may only have one beacon active at a time.

A twisted chamber of throbbing volcanic glass and obsidian curves. Every word spoken here prompts a mocking retort from somewhere out of eyesight. Reflections display perverse mockeries of those who cast them. Smooth surfaces pierce like knives and sharp edges are dull to the touch. The spirit who forged this room was a sadist beyond compare. Destroy a single structure you have built in Dis and do not recover its building materials. The spiteful echo writhes in glee. Select any building that has been identified from an enemy faction. Destroy it.
